pink.note: The brand's real, dominant color — used directly and exactly everywhere pink appears (buttons, badges, emphasis text, icons, step numbers, testimonial stars/initials, hover states). Restoring it in full, in place of a darker accessible stand-in, was an explicit client decision that prioritizes brand fidelity over WCAG AA on those elements (2.82:1 on white).

pink-dark.note: Kept only for accessibility-feature chrome invisible during normal browsing — the keyboard focus ring and the skip-to-content link. Not used anywhere in visible-by-default content.

teal.note: The brand's real, dominant teal — used directly everywhere teal appears (icons, fills, badge text, section markers, testimonial-avatar initials). Same brand-fidelity decision and same accepted contrast tradeoff as the pink (1.87:1 on white).

teal-dark.note: Currently unused in visible content; kept as a token in case a future accessibility-only feature needs it, mirroring pink-dark.

title.purpose: Card titles: services, steps, testimonials, info cards.

body-sm.purpose: Secondary running copy: FAQ answers, testimonial text, legal/policy paragraphs (15px, not an arbitrary 14px).

label.purpose: Badges, eyebrow step numbers, info-card labels, meta text.

shadows.name: floating-stat-card
shadows.purpose: Floating stat cards over the hero photo.

shadows.name: scrolled-header
shadows.purpose: Sticky header once scrolled past the top.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for menu, nav underline, FAQ chevron, testimonial track transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The WhatsApp booking CTA, repeated at every major section.
components.html: Agende sua consulta pelo WhatsApp
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; background: #FD6498; color: #fff; transition: transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { transform: scale(0.98); outline: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary fallback action (phone call) alongside the primary WhatsApp CTA.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; background: transparent; border: 1.5px solid #FD6498; color: #FD6498; transition: transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover, .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { transform: scale(0.98); outline: none; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Section-opening pill label (e.g. "Saúde da Mulher", "Sobre a Clínica").
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 6px 16px; border-radius: 50px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.5px; background: rgba(253,100,152,0.1); color: #FD6498; }

components.name: Service Card
components.description: Icon + title + description card used across the services grid.
components.html:
Consulta de Enfermagem Ginecológica

Avaliação, orientações e acompanhamento voltados à saúde íntima e ao bem-estar feminino.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 20px; padding: 28px 24px; border: 1px solid #E9EDF2; } .ds-card-icon { width: 48px; height: 48px; border-radius: 14px; background: rgba(253,100,152,0.08); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: #FD6498; margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-card h3 { font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; color: #232323; margin: 0 0 8px; line-height: 1.35; } .ds-card p { font-size: 14px; color: #525C68; line-height: 1.65; margin: 0; }

components.name: FAQ Accordion Item
components.description: Expandable question/answer row; open state washes to a pink tint.
components.html: O agendamento é feito pelo WhatsApp.
components.css: .ds-faq { background: #FFF0F5; border-radius: 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(253,100,152,0.25); overflow: hidden; } .ds-faq-q { width: 100%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 20px 24px; background: none; border: none; text-align: left; gap: 16px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; color: #232323; cursor: pointer; } .ds-faq-icon { width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 50%; background: #FD6498; color: #fff; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; transform: rotate(180deg); } .ds-faq-a p { padding: 0 24px 20px; font-size: 15px; color: #525C68; line-height: 1.75; margin: 0; }

components.name: WhatsApp Floating Button
components.description: Fixed bottom-right button that expands to reveal its label on hover.
components.html: Falar pelo WhatsApp
components.css: .ds-wa-float { position: fixed; bottom: 28px; right: 28px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0; background: #25D366; color: #fff; border-radius: 100px; padding: 14px; text-decoration: none; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap; transition: padding 0.3s, transform 0.2s; } .ds-wa-float-text { max-width: 0; overflow: hidden; transition: max-width 0.3s; display: inline-block; } .ds-wa-float:hover, .ds-wa-float:focus-visible { padding: 14px 20px; gap: 10px; transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-wa-float:hover .ds-wa-float-text, .ds-wa-float:focus-visible .ds-wa-float-text { max-width: 160px; }

narrative.overview: Clínica do Bem's interface reads like a conversation with someone who has your back — soft, unhurried, never clinical-cold. Warm Blossom Pink carries emotional warmth and is the page's dominant voice; Calm Sage Teal sits underneath as a quieter counterpoint, standing in for the clinic's broader nursing-care work. Nothing on the page shouts: rounded pill buttons, soft gradients, and generous whitespace all work together to feel like a welcoming room rather than a waiting one. This system explicitly rejects the sterile, corporate-hospital look (stock photography, blue-and-white palettes, impersonal tone) and the generic local-business template feel.
keyCharacteristics: Soft, rounded geometry everywhere (pills, 20–28px card corners, circular icon badges) — no hard edges.
Warm Blossom Pink leads; Calm Sage Teal supports; both stay pastel-tinted rather than saturated.
Flat surfaces with atmosphere carried by soft gradient washes and blurred radial glows, not hard shadows.
Generous section rhythm (100px vertical padding as the default beat) that varies deliberately at the page's open and close.

rules.name: The Tint-Don't-Saturate Rule
rules.body: Both accent colors appear far more often as a soft tint (backgrounds, badges, gradient washes at 6–20% opacity) than as a solid fill.

rules.name: The Brand-Fidelity Rule
rules.body: The original pink (#FD6498) and teal (#7FCCB8) are used exactly as-is everywhere — buttons, badges, icons, emphasis text, hover states, decorative marks. A prior pass had rerouted much of this to darker AA-compliant variants; the client explicitly asked for the original colors back in full, choosing brand identity over that compliance gain. Known, accepted tradeoff: contrast now sits below AA wherever the base colors carry content. pink-dark remains only for the keyboard focus ring and skip-link, both invisible during normal browsing.

rules.name: The One Big Statement Rule
rules.body: Only the hero H1 uses the display size. Section titles read as a clear step down so repeating six section headers down the page never reads as six equally loud shouts.

rules.name: The Border-Before-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Cards default to a 1px border, not a shadow. A shadow is earned only by elements meant to feel physically separated from the page (photos, floating stat cards, the map, the scrolled header).

dos: Do keep pink as the dominant, teal as the supporting accent — never let teal outweigh pink in a single section.
Do use the pill shape (50px radius) for every button and badge; it's the system's single strongest shape signal.
Do default new cards to a 1px solid border line, not a shadow.
Do cap section title size at clamp(26px,2.6vw,38px) — the hero H1 is the only element allowed the larger display scale.
Do write copy around sensitive topics (ISTs, gynecological procedures) with the same unhurried, non-judgmental warmth as the rest of the page.
Do use the base pink/teal (#FD6498/#7FCCB8) for everything visible — buttons, badges, icons, emphasis text, hover states, decorative marks — even where contrast sits below AA.
Do keep pink-dark reserved for the two accessibility-only features invisible in normal browsing: the keyboard focus ring and skip-link.
donts: Don't introduce a cold, corporate-hospital aesthetic — no stock photography, no blue-and-white clinical palettes, no impersonal tone.
Don't let the page read as a generic local-business template.
Don't add hard drop shadows to resting cards; shadows are reserved for the photo frame, floating stat cards, scrolled header, mobile menu, and map only.
Don't use sharp corners anywhere — the system has no 0px or small-radius (2–8px) elements; the floor is 14px.
Don't dilute the WhatsApp CTA's visual priority — it must stay the single most prominent action in the hero, services, "como funciona," and final CTA sections.

Don't use pink-dark/teal-dark in any visible-by-default content — reserved exclusively for the focus ring and skip-link. Reintroducing it elsewhere is exactly the change the client asked to have reverted.
